questions.q: Which of the following is a major source of water resource?
Describe the process of groundwater recharge. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Groundwater recharge is the process by which water moves downward from the Earth's surface to the water table, recharging the aquifers. This can occur through infiltration of rainfall or surface water, or through artificial means such as injection wells.

questions.q: What are the main causes of water pollution in India? (10 marks)
questions.answer: The main causes of water pollution in India include industrial waste, agricultural runoff, domestic sewage, and lack of proper waste management. Industrial waste from factories and manufacturing processes releases toxic chemicals and heavy metals into water bodies, while agricultural runoff from fertilizers and pesticides contributes to eutrophication. Domestic sewage from households and municipalities also contaminates water sources, and the lack of proper waste management facilities allows pollutants to enter water bodies.

questions.q: Water is a renewable resource, but it is also a _______________________ resource.
True or False: Rainwater harvesting is a traditional practice in India.
What is the main purpose of the Damodar Valley Project? (5 marks)
questions.answer: The main purpose of the Damodar Valley Project is to control floods, generate hydroelectric power, and provide irrigation facilities to the surrounding areas.

questions.q: Describe the importance of water conservation in India. (10 marks)
questions.answer: Water conservation is essential in India due to the country's increasing population and decreasing water resources. Effective water management practices, such as rainwater harvesting and efficient irrigation systems, can help reduce water waste and ensure sustainable use of this vital resource. Additionally, water conservation helps to prevent water pollution, supports agricultural productivity, and maintains ecosystem health.

questions.q: What is the role of groundwater in the water cycle? (5 marks)
questions.answer: Groundwater plays a crucial role in the water cycle by storing and releasing water into streams, rivers, and lakes. Groundwater is recharged through infiltration of rainfall and surface water, and it is released through springs and wells, contributing to the flow of surface water.

questions.q: Explain the concept of water scarcity in India. (10 marks)
questions.answer: Water scarcity in India refers to a situation where the demand for water exceeds its availability, leading to a shortage of this vital resource. Factors contributing to water scarcity in India include population growth, climate change, inefficient water management practices, and lack of investment in water infrastructure. As a result, many parts of the country face severe water shortages, affecting agriculture, industry, and human consumption.

questions.q: What are the main components of a watershed? (5 marks)
questions.answer: The main components of a watershed include the land surface, hills, valleys, rivers, lakes, and groundwater. These components interact and influence each other, affecting the water cycle, soil quality, and ecosystem health within the watershed.
